name: Weather sentry
description: Schedule pulls a still from an outdoor camera (parking lot, yard, perimeter) and asks Gemini to judge current conditions and visibility. Posts to Helix so ops teams can alert on developing hazards — heavy rain, fog rolling in, snow accumulation — without paying for a weather service or running a separate sensor stack.
summary: Schedule every 30 min → Gemini still-image (JSON) → Helix weather event

config.prompt:
You are judging weather conditions from an outdoor security camera frame. Look at the sky, ground, surfaces, and atmosphere.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object - no prose, no code fence - with exactly three keys:
  "conditions": one of "clear", "cloudy", "overcast", "rain", "snow", "fog", "storm", or "unknown". Pick the dominant condition. Use "unknown" if the camera is indoors / blocked / nighttime with no visual signal.
  "visibility": one of "good" (you can see across the frame normally), "reduced" (haze / light rain / partial fog), "poor" (heavy precipitation / dense fog / sub-100ft visibility).
  "hazards":    short comma-separated string of active hazards you observe (e.g. "ice on pavement, snow accumulation, standing water, blowing debris"), or "none" when nothing notable.

Do not infer beyond what's visible:
  - Don't predict (no "looks like it might rain").
  - Don't quote temperatures or wind speed - you don't have sensors.
  - Nighttime with clear sky should be "clear" + "good" visibility, NOT "unknown".

Examples:
  {"conditions": "clear", "visibility": "good", "reasoning_omit": null, "hazards": "none"}
  {"conditions": "snow", "visibility": "reduced", "hazards": "snow accumulation on driveway, slippery surface likely"}
  {"conditions": "fog", "visibility": "poor", "hazards": "dense fog, vehicles barely visible 30 feet out"}
  {"conditions": "rain", "visibility": "reduced", "hazards": "standing water near loading dock"}
